primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of an intragroup dividend receivable as a hedged item for foreign currency risk hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Paragraph 80 establishes that only assets, liabilities, firm commitments or highly probable forecast transactions involving a party external to the entity can be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements, with a specific exception for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items when FX differences affect consolidated profit or loss.
IFRS 9 maintains the external party requirement for hedged items and provides the equivalent exception for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements.

supporting_authority.reason: IAS 21 explains that intragroup monetary items are not eliminated on consolidation when the entities have different functional currencies, which is the condition under which the foreign currency risk exception applies.

IFRIC 16 clarifies that hedge accounting for net investment hedges only applies when net assets are included in financial statements, but this is peripheral as dividend receivables do not represent net investment hedges.

peripheral_authority.reason: Detailed hedge accounting mechanics for fair value, cash flow, and net investment hedges are not relevant to determining initial eligibility of intragroup items as hedged items.

treatment_families.family: Ineligibility of intragroup items as hedged items under the external party requirement
Foreign currency risk exception allowing hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items

approaches.label: Fair value hedge exclusion for intragroup item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under the external party requirement in IAS 39 paragraph 80 and IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5, an intragroup dividend receivable does not qualify as a hedged item because intragroup items are eliminated in consolidation and involve parties within the same reporting entity, not external parties.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under the exception in IAS 39 paragraph 80 and IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6, the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item (such as a dividend receivable) may qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements if the item is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity receiving it and the foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss.
